title: Crystallisation of Enterovirus coxsackievirus A16 2A protease
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9rjvo","text":"Picornaviridae coxsackievirus A16 is the causative agent of paediatric hand-foot-and-mouth disease, and a target for pandemic preparedness due to the risk of higher order complications in a large-scale outbreak. The 2A protease of the virus is responsible for self-cleavage from the poly protein, allowing for correct folding and assembly of capsid proteins in the final stages of viral replication. Inhibition deranges capsid folding and assembly, preventing formation of mature virions in host cells and making the protease a valuable target for antiviral activity. This protocol was used to grow coxsackievirus A16 crystals that were applied high-throughput crystallographic fragment screening on the target.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Protein and buffer requirements: 43.2 µL 20 mg/mL Sample 3.36 mL, Crystallization screen 14.4 µL Sample seeds, dilution 1:1000

Dispense 35 µL, Crystallisation screen into SwissCI 3 lens plate reservoir wells using a 100 µl multi-channel pipette. Dispense 150 nL 20 mg/mL Sample to each lens using the SPT mosquito. Dispense 150 nL, Crystallisation screen to each lens using the SPT mosquito. Dispense 50 nL, Seeds to each lens using the SPT mosquito. Drop ratio: 3:3:1 ratio (150 nl Sample : 150 nl reservoir solution: 50 nl seeds) Final drop volume: 350 nl

Crystallisation screen composition: 13.5 % w/v PEG 20000 0.1 Molarity (M) MES pH 6.7 Stock solutions used: 1 Molarity (M) MES pH 6.7 50% w/v PEG 20000 The crystallisation screen can be stored in a duran bottle or aliquoted into 96 deep well block for easy dispensing into SwissCI 3 lens plates. For long term storage keep the Crystallisation screen in the fridge at 4°C.

Crystal typically form after ~24hrs Crystals typically reach their maximum size after ~36 h. Morphology: typically rectangles. Size: ~75 μm in length and ~10 μm in width, depth of the crystals is ~10 μm, giving arectangular appearance Average resolution: 1.6 Å Space group: C2 Unit cell: 86 Å, 57 Å, 32 Å 90°, 95°, 90° An example of a drop containing Coxsackievirus A16 crystals.

Incubate at 20 °C for 24:00:00, h in Formulatrix Rock Imager. Imaging Schedule : The first images are taken after 12hrs and the imaging schedule follows a Fibonacci sequence of days for further collections.

Formulatrix Rock Imager (or incubator of choice) SPT mosquito Mosquito HV (High Volume 16-Channel Robotic Liquid Handler, SPT LabTech, 3097-01057, https://www.sptlabtech.com/products/liquid-handling/mosquito-hv/ ) P100 8 multi-channel pipette SwissCI 3 lens plate
Prepare seed stock: Diamond XChem Seeding Protocol by Peter Marples 1: 1000 dilution Sample seeds

Diamond Light Source Unattended Data Collection (UDC) Data Collection Temperature: 100K Detector: DECTRIS EIGER2 X 9M Beamline: I04-1 Wavelength: 0.9212 Å Resolution (Å): 1.21 Beam Size (μm): 60 X 50 Number of images: 3600 Oscillation: 0.10° Exposure (s): 0.0020 Transmission (%): 100 Flux (ph/s): 9.50e+11

The protein used for crystallisation was expressed and purified using the following protocol. Enterovirus coxsackievirus A16 2A protease small scale expression and purification protocol by korvus.wang
creator: Peter Marples
